reasoning_model_1: Model 1 exhibits a significant content accuracy and fluency failure at the very beginning of the audio. The script's opening word 'Happy' is mispronounced/stuttered as 'H- Any' (Timestamp: 00:00-00:01). This results in the phrase sounding like 'H- Any New Year everyone,' which distorts the meaning and breaks the natural flow of speech. According to the evaluation criteria, if the first two points (Content/Fluency) show obvious flaws, the score is penalized. The hesitation and mispronunciation prevent the model from effectively conveying a confident 'loudly volume,' as the delivery sounds unsure and broken.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 delivers the script with perfect content accuracy. The sentence 'Happy New Year everyone! May all your wishes come true!' is pronounced clearly and correctly from start to finish (Timestamp: 00:00-00:04). The fluency is natural, with no stutters or mechanical artifacts. By satisfying the content and fluency requirements perfectly, the model provides a solid foundation to express the 'loudly volume' feature. The delivery is consistent and aligns well with the celebratory nature of the text.
model_comparison: Comparing the two models, there is a distinct difference in basic performance. Model 1 fails the fundamental requirement of content accuracy by stuttering and mispronouncing the first word ('H- Any' instead of 'Happy'). This error severely impacts naturalness and makes the paralinguistic expression of 'loudness' ineffective. Model 2, on the other hand, follows the script perfectly with natural prosody. Since Model 2 is fluent and accurate while Model 1 has major issues, Model 2 demonstrates superior quality.
